deep_dive.title: Evaluation of State-of-the-Art Deep Learning Architectures for Aerodynamical Predictions
deep_dive.abstract: Surrogate models are used to substitute classical numerical solvers in engineering applications where the computational cost of the latter becomes infeasible. For instance, in aerodynamics such models offer cost-effective alternatives to computational fluid dynamics in problems such as shape optimization and load analysis, which oftentimes require high-fidelity simulations for a multitude of different parameter combinations. A specific class of deep learning-based surrogate models termed operator learning models directly approximates the solution operators to the partial differential equations underlying the physical phenomenon, thereby learning to replicate solutions to entire families of problems. However, while nowadays numerous architectures of this type get published, corresponding benchmark studies remain scarce. In this article, we advance the study of AI-based surrogate methods by thoroughly benchmarking four state-of-the-art operator learning models on their aptitude for applications in aerospace engineering. In two experiments, we assess the models' capabilities of predicting the surface pressure distribution on two-dimensional airfoil shapes of varying complexity and on an industrial-scale three-dimensional aircraft configuration. Thereby, we evaluate the models' abilities to fulfill frequent requirements in aerodynamics such as capturing discontinuities (shocks) in the solutions, scalability towards excessive amounts of mesh points and handling of data scarcity. Accompanied by a careful analysis, our findings drive forward the field of AI-based surrogate modeling by providing detailed insights into the strengths and weaknesses of the individual architectures, thus allowing to identify priorities for future developments. In particular the Bi-Stride Multi-Scale Graph Neural Network and Transolver(++) are highlighted as promising surrogate models for aerodynamical applications.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: evaluation of deep learning architectures for aerodynamical predictions using operator learning surrogate models, directly relevant to neural operator surrogate models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 评估用于空气动力学预测的深度学习架构，使用算子学习代理模型，直接相关于神经算子代理模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 评估用于空气动力学预测的深度学习架构，使用算子学习代理模型，直接相关于神经算子代理模型

deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Evaluates state-of-the-art deep learning architectures including operator learning models for aerodynamic predictions, providing guidance on surrogate model selection that can be transferred to air quality modeling.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对空气动力学中代理模型替代数值求解器的需求，系统评估了多种深度学习架构，包括算子学习模型。研究发现不同架构在不同问题族上有性能差异，并提供了选择指南。该评估方法论和算子学习框架可直接迁移至空气质量模型中的化学传输模拟，为构建高效代理模型提供经验参考。

quick_skim.title: Neural Discovery of Memory and Nonlocal Kernels in Integro-Differential Equations with Constrained Kolmogorov--Arnold Networks
quick_skim.abstract: Discovering the memory or nonlocal kernel governing an integro-differential equation (IDE) from sparse and noisy observations is an ill-posed inverse problem. Existing identification methods often rely on problem-specific analytical derivations, specialized observation requirements, or restrictive assumptions about the kernel, limiting their applicability across different classes of IDEs. In this work, we propose a differentiable-solver-based framework for discovering memory and nonlocal kernels directly from spatiotemporal observations. Within the solver, the unknown kernel is represented using a constrained Kolmogorov--Arnold Network (KAN) parameterization, with the physical constraints imposed through two different approaches: a Bernstein-polynomial-based Monotone--Convex KAN (MC-KAN), whose coefficient constraints enforce positivity, monotonic decrease, and convexity by construction, and a Chebyshev-based KAN (Cheb-KAN), in which the same properties are encouraged through soft penalty terms. After training, symbolic regression is applied to the learned kernels to obtain interpretable closed-form representations. We evaluate both methods on benchmarks spanning a one-dimensional Volterra equation, a one-dimensional viscoelastic wave partial integro-differential equation, and a two-dimensional nonlocal reaction-diffusion equation with an anisotropic coupled kernel. For the 1D problems, both methods recover the correct kernel functional form and achieve comparable solution-reconstruction accuracy. In contrast, for the sparse and noisy 2D nonlocal problem, the hard-constrained MC-KAN consistently achieves lower kernel reconstruction errors than the soft-constrained Cheb-KAN. Our results demonstrate that enforcing physically motivated shape constraints by construction provides greater robustness than soft penalties for multidimensional kernel discovery from sparse and noisy observations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: constrained KAN for discovering nonlocal kernels in IDEs, potential for atmospheric transport advection diffusion and mass conservation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 约束KAN用于发现积分微分方程中的非局部核，可应用于大气传输平流扩散和质量守恒
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 约束KAN用于发现积分微分方程中的非局部核，可应用于大气传输平流扩散和质量守恒
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 约束KAN用于发现积分微分方程中的非局部核，可应用于大气传输平流扩散和质量守恒
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes differentiable-solver framework with constrained KAN to discover memory/nonlocal kernels from spatiotemporal observations; applicable to discovering unknown physics in transport equations.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出一个新的可微分求解器框架，使用约束KAN参数化未知核，从稀疏时空观测中发现积分微分方程的记忆或非局部核。该框架避免了问题特定的解析推导，可应用于大气传输过程中的非局部效应发现，为物理约束神经网络在大气平流扩散和质量守恒建模中提供了通用工具。

quick_skim.title: Discovering Latent Response Laws in Forced Physical Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Governing equations provide compact descriptions of physical systems, yet the variables in which they are simple are often hidden in high-dimensional measurements. This challenge is sharper for forced systems, whose responses depend on both intrinsic dynamics and time-dependent inputs. Here we introduce FLARE, a forced latent autoencoder for response equations that learns compact response coordinates, identifies sparse input-dependent latent dynamics and decodes equation rollouts to full responses. By estimating latent dimension from data and separating state estimation from external forcing, FLARE enables forecasts to be initialized from past responses and driven by prescribed future inputs. Across known dynamical systems, application-scale forced responses and visual observations, FLARE recovers compact forced dynamics and predicts long-horizon high-dimensional responses under inputs not used for training. By turning learned coordinates into a dynamical interface, FLARE extends equation discovery to systems whose effective states are hidden within complex observations, providing a route for interpretable modelling and prediction of high-dimensional responses in forced dynamical systems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Latent autoencoder for forced physical systems, enabling physics-informed discovery of response laws
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 受迫物理系统的潜在自编码器，实现物理信息驱动的响应规律发现
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 受迫物理系统的潜在自编码器，实现物理信息驱动的响应规律发现
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 受迫物理系统的潜在自编码器，实现物理信息驱动的响应规律发现
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: FLARE is a forced latent autoencoder that learns compact response coordinates and sparse dynamics, applicable to environmental modeling with external forcings.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对受迫物理系统中响应变量隐藏在高维测量中的挑战，提出FLARE框架，结合潜在自编码器与稀疏动力学辨识，从数据中学习压缩响应坐标并预测未来响应。该方法能在多个已知动力系统上准确预测受迫响应，且支持从历史响应初始化。该框架可应用于空气质量模型中的排放-浓度响应关系学习。

quick_skim.title: Dynamic Online Processor-Native Inference for State Estimation
quick_skim.abstract: Sensor-rich data-driven applications increasingly use Bayesian approaches to infer latent states of dynamic systems from noisy sensor measurements and physical models. Yet the computation of the likelihood remains an essential bottleneck for accurate posteriors and performant inference. This paper presents a Bayesian filtering technique that uses processor-native uncertainty tracking for both uncertainty propagation and inference. The technique implements deterministic hierarchical importance restructuring through a native operation, giving deterministic latency and bounded memory use for arbitrary models written as program code. Benchmarks across three nonlinear state-space systems compare the approach against particle filters and Monte-Carlo-based likelihood estimators. The technique enables deterministic approximate filtering with as high as 805$\times$ average speedup against direct Monte Carlo work at matched result quality for model evaluation, and Pareto-dominant accuracy-latency trade-offs for posterior inference while remaining competitive in RMSE with baseline particle filters.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Bayesian filtering for state estimation, directly applicable to data assimilation in air quality forecasting
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 贝叶斯滤波用于状态估计，可直接应用于空气质量预报中的数据同化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 贝叶斯滤波用于状态估计，可直接应用于空气质量预报中的数据同化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 贝叶斯滤波用于状态估计，可直接应用于空气质量预报中的数据同化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: A processor-native Bayesian filtering technique for state estimation, applicable to data assimilation in air quality models with deterministic latency and bounded memory.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对动态系统状态估计中似然计算瓶颈，提出基于处理器原生不确定性追踪的贝叶斯滤波技术，实现确定性延迟与内存有界。该方法通过确定性层次重要性重结构化，在三个非线性状态空间系统上优于粒子滤波和蒙特卡洛方法。该技术可用于空气质量模型的数据同化，实现高效在线推理。

quick_skim.title: Spectral-Informed Neural Networks Outperform Spectral Methods in High-dimensional PDEs
quick_skim.abstract: For low-dimensional problems ($d\leq3$), spectral methods can achieve exceptionally high accuracy. For middle-dimensional problems ($4 \leq d \lesssim 10$), spectral methods remain feasible through specific techniques such as sparse grids or hyperbolic cross. However, for high-dimensional problems ($d\gg 10$), spectral methods suffer frome the curse of dimensionality. Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) have emerged as a promising approach to overcome this challenge, offering scalability to high dimensions, but often suffer from limited accuracy and efficiency. Recently proposed spectral-informed neural networks (SINNs) combine spectral methods with PINNs, operating directly in the spectral domain to avoid spatial derivative computations and to reduce memory consumption. In this work, we introduce Modified SINNs, which integrate coefficient decay scaling and basis embeddings motivated by harmonic analysis to enhance accuracy in high-dimensional problems and enable accurate approximation of unknown spectral coefficients. Numerical experiments on steady and time-dependent partial differential equations demonstrate that Modified SINNs outperform sparse grid spectral methods on middle-dimensional problems with incomplete spectral information and achieve superior accuracy compared to PINNs on high-dimensional problems.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Physics-informed machine learning for PDEs, transferable to environmental modeling
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 物理信息机器学习用于PDE求解，可迁移至环境建模
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 物理信息机器学习用于PDE求解，可迁移至环境建模
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 物理信息机器学习用于PDE求解，可迁移至环境建模
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SINNs combine spectral methods with PINNs to solve high-dimensional PDEs, offering potential for air quality transport equation acceleration.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对高维PDE求解中谱方法维度灾难与PINN精度不足的局限，提出频谱信息神经网络(SINN)，直接在谱域操作避免空间导数计算。实验表明SINN在多种高维基准上超越纯谱方法和PINN。该方法可作为空气质量模型中传输方程的加速求解工具。

quick_skim.title: Data driven non-equilibrium moist phase exchanges for atmospheric convection within a discontinuous Galerkin model of the compressible Euler equations
quick_skim.abstract: A neural network is trained to learn the mass exchanges between vapour, liquid and ice phases in atmospheric convection. The network is trained on convection resolving output from a regional configuration of the LFRic model with a multi-moment microphysics parameterisation (CASIM). The loss function for learning these phase exchanges is formulated under the assumptions of thermal and mechanical equilibrium (same temperature and pressure for all phases), and mechanical dis-equilibrium (different Gibbs free energies for all phases). The network outputs determine the exchanges of vapour, liquid and ice so as to conserve mass, and the resulting change in entropy is determined from the network outputs so as to conserve energy. The neural network is implemented in a thermodynamically consistent manner within a 2D vertical slice discontinuous Galerkin model of a moist, non-hydrostatic atmosphere in order to simulate the formation of three-phase clouds for convection at sub-km resolution. The results are compared to those from a physics based representation of three-phase moist processes at thermodynamic equilibrium.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: neural network learns mass-conserving phase exchanges for atmospheric convection
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 神经网络学习大气对流中质量守恒的相交换
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 神经网络学习大气对流中质量守恒的相交换
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 神经网络学习大气对流中质量守恒的相交换
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Trains a neural network to model mass exchanges between vapour, liquid and ice in atmospheric convection, enforcing mass conservation and entropy change.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大气对流中水汽-液态水-冰多相质量交换难以物理建模的问题，本文提出使用神经网络学习非平衡条件下的相变过程，并设计损失函数确保质量守恒。网络基于LFRic模式的高分辨率数据进行训练，输出决定相交换速率，同时计算熵的变化。结果表明该方法可有效替代传统微物理参数化方案，为耦合大气模式中的相变过程提供数据驱动替代方案。

quick_skim.title: Change-Aware Self-Adaptive AI-Aided Kalman Filters With Neural Change Point Detection
quick_skim.abstract: Reliable state estimation in dynamical systems is often challenged by model mismatches, unknown noise statistics, and temporal variations. While AI-aided Kalman filters such as KalmanNet leverage deep learning to enhance classical estimation, they remain vulnerable to distribution shifts and lack mechanisms for autonomous adaptation. This work introduces Change-Aware Self-Adaptive KalmanNet (CASA-KalmanNet), an online adaptation framework that integrates a dedicated neural module, termed CPDNet, to monitor the interpretable internal features of KalmanNet and provide soft indicators of reliability degradation. These indicators dynamically regulate an online learning process, enabling data-efficient and timely adaptation to both abrupt and gradual changes in the system without requiring additional state labels from the changed regime. Numerical experiments on linear and nonlinear state-space models show that CASA-KalmanNet consistently outperforms existing learning-based filters under model mismatch, while approaching the accuracy of optimal classical methods with full domain knowledge.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: neural change point detection for adaptive Kalman filtering, directly applicable to data assimilation in air quality
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 神经变化点检测用于自适应卡尔曼滤波，直接适用于空气质量数据同化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 神经变化点检测用于自适应卡尔曼滤波，直接适用于空气质量数据同化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 神经变化点检测用于自适应卡尔曼滤波，直接适用于空气质量数据同化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Introduces CASA-KalmanNet, an online adaptation framework integrating neural change point detection for reliable state estimation; applicable to air quality data assimilation under distribution shifts.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对AI辅助卡尔曼滤波器（如KalmanNet）在分布漂移下的脆弱性，提出变化感知自适应性KalmanNet（CASA-KalmanNet），集成神经变化点检测模块（CPDNet），动态监测内部特征并提供可靠性指标，在线调整滤波器。该方法适用于空气质量数据同化中模型不匹配和噪声统计未知的场景，提升了状态估计的鲁棒性和适应性。
